title: Project-A research assistant repository
raw_summary: An AI assistant repository pattern combining web search, task management, and research paper analysis.
user_note: Saved as a nearby workflow signal for research-plus-task tooling, with risk of drifting into a generic assistant.

evidence_notes.claim: Research assistant projects cluster around source collection plus task workflow features.
evidence_notes.source_quote_or_detail: Repository summary references web search, task management, and research paper analysis in one assistant workflow.

evidence_notes.claim: The repo is only indirect evidence for an experiment-selection wedge.
evidence_notes.source_quote_or_detail: The current signal can support a candidate card but does not prove demand for a sharper decision-quality tool.

risks: Could become a generic assistant instead of a decision-quality tool

review_notes: Relevant to experiment selection because it shows adjacent demand for research workflow tooling.
Duplicate check completed: no existing canonical source match in fixture set.

title: Code as Agent Harness
raw_summary: Frames agents as code, harness, and evaluable execution units rather than freeform summaries.
user_note: Saved because it supports making experiment briefs concrete through harness and acceptance criteria fields.

evidence_notes.claim: Agent candidates should be evaluated through executable harnesses and acceptance criteria.
evidence_notes.source_quote_or_detail: Fixture summary frames agents as code/harness/evaluable execution units.

evidence_notes.claim: This source has direct fit with the proposed experiment brief template.
evidence_notes.source_quote_or_detail: The candidate requires harness, fixture, and acceptance criteria fields before prototyping.

risks: May overfit to agent experiments before validating broader research workflows

title: Structured signals outperform generative matching in reviewer selection
raw_summary: Simple statistical or structured representations can outperform generative AI for matching and scoring tasks.
user_note: Saved to justify explicit scoring dimensions before LLM-only ranking is added.

evidence_notes.claim: Structured scoring can be a useful baseline before LLM recommendations.
evidence_notes.source_quote_or_detail: Fixture summary says structured representations can outperform generative AI for matching/scoring tasks.

evidence_notes.claim: Manual sources may not always provide enough structured metadata.
evidence_notes.source_quote_or_detail: Candidate risk notes that structured signals may be unavailable for manually entered sources.

risks: Structured signals may be unavailable for manually entered sources

review_notes: Relevant to experiment selection because it supports visible deterministic scoring dimensions.
Duplicate check completed: no existing canonical source match in fixture set.

title: Manual note: weekly research digest intake
raw_summary: A manually entered idea to turn a weekly research digest into experiment candidates.
user_note: No URL because this is an offline planning note; next research step is to attach at least one primary source before candidate extraction.

evidence_notes.claim: User intent exists, but primary evidence is missing.
evidence_notes.source_quote_or_detail: Manual note has an experiment angle but no linked paper, repo, or article yet.

risks: Missing primary source evidence before candidate extraction

review_notes: No URL rationale accepted for intake only; do not extract a candidate until primary evidence is attached.
